MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-15-2011 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF DOUGLAS SMITH'S MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS & PERMIT TRANSFER OF THE PROPERTY & FOR ATTYS FEES. THIS MATTER WAS CONT'D FROM DEC-15-2011 FOR SOMEONE TO APPEAR ON BEHALF OF MR. ANTHONY. THERE BEING NO OTHER APPEARANCES, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: GRANTED IN PART & DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFFS STATE THE 7 ITEMS OF RELIEF THEY REQUEST ON PAGE 9 OF THEIR MEMO FILED WITH THEIR MOVING PAPERS. PLAINTIFFS ARE ENTITLED TO EXPUNGEMENT OF THE LIS PENDENS (ITEM 5) SINCE DEFENDANT 300 PARKING ST LLC STATES IT NO LONGER IS THE OWNER OF THE DISPUTED NOTE & IT HAS REMOVED THE LIS PENDENS. ALL OTHER ITEMS OF RELIEF SOUGHT BY PLAINTIFFS ARE DENIED. PLAINTIFFS PROVIDE NO AUTHORITY THAT THIS COURT IS PERMITTED TO GRANT ITEMS 1, 2, 3, 4 & 6 ABSENT A TRIAL, A RECOGNIZED DISPOSITIVE MOTION, AN OSC OR A PI MOTION &, EVEN IF THE COURT COULD GRANT SUCH RELIEF, PLAINTIFFS HAVE NOT PROVIDED ANY ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT THE RELIEF. THE DECLARATION OF MR. BONIS, WHILE IT STATES THAT IS MADE ON PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE, QUITE OBVIOUSLY IS NOT - AT LEAST AS TO THE PURPORTED FACTS AS TO THE ASSERTED ILLEGALITY OF THE DISPUTED NOTE. PLAINTIFFS ARE NOT ABLE TO RECOVER THEIR FEES FOR THIS MOTION (ITEM 7), EVEN THE PORTION OF THOSE FEES RELATING TO THE LIS PENDENS SINCE PLAINTIFFS HAVE PROVIDED NO EVIDENCE OF THE AMOUNT OF THOSE FEES. PLAINTIFFS' MEMO STATES THAT A "FEE DECLARATION" IS "SUBMITTED SEPARATELY, BUT THE COURT HAS NOT RECEIVED ONE. NOTWITHSTANDING THE DENIAL OF ALL BUT ITEM 5, IT SEEMS APPARENT THAT SOMETHING IS VERY WRONG WITH REGARD TO THE DISPUTED NOTE & ITS SERIAL ASSIGNMENTS & THAT THE PARTIES AND COUNSEL ARE ADVISED THAT THEY MUST SCRUPULOUSLY REFRAIN FROM ANY FRAUDULENT OR OTHER ILLEGAL CONDUCT. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. THE COURT ISSUED A TRO AND OSC AS TO JOSEPH ANTHONY, NOTE PURCHASING CORP & J. ANTHONY INVESTMENTS & SET A HEARING FOR JAN-5-2012 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT 302. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; NOT REPORT
MINI MINUTES FOR DEC-16-2011 03:00 PM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT WILLIAM GARLOCK AND GFT PROPERTIES LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SIXTH CAUSE OF ACTION AND OVERRULE WITH TEN DAYS TO ANSWER AS TO ALL OTHER CAUSES OF ACTION. PLAINTIFFS CONCEDE THE LACK OF MERIT OF THEIR ABUSE OF PROCESS CLAIM. ALL OTHER CLAIMS ARE ADEQUATELY PLED, DO NOT REQUIRE ANY OTHER PERSONS TO BE ADDED AS PARTIES FOR THEM TO BE PROPERLY ADJUDICATED AND ARE NOT THE SAME AS THE CLAIMS ALLEGED IN CASE NUMBER 502878, ALTHOUGH THE TWO CASES SHOULD PROBABLY BE CONSOLIDATED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

Plaintiffs' Order To Show Cause
Off Calendar Jan-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF DOUGLAS A. SMITH'S ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE.
Appearances: Alan N. Herzfeld of Goldstein Gellman Melbostad Harris & McSparran LLP (673.5600) specially appearing for J. Anthony; Peter Bonis of the Law Offices of Peter H. Bonis (925.287.6428) and Carlos A. Alvarez of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (421.3400) for plaintiffs; Andrew Cross of Garlock & Company (650.371.9412) for defendants Garlock and GFT.
Discussed and the court ruled as follows: Preliminary injunction granted. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-05-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT WILLIAM GARLOCK AND GFT PROPERTIES LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.
Appearances: Peter Bonis of the Law Offices of Peter H. Bonis (925.287.6428) and Carlos A. Alvarez of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (421.3400) for plaintiffs; Andrew Cross of Garlock & Company (650.371.9412) for defendants Garlock and GFT.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action and overrule with ten days to answer as to all other causes of action. Plaintiffs concede the lack of merit of their abuse of process claim. All other claims are adequately pled, do not require any other persons to be added as parties for them to be properly adjudicated and are not the same as the claims alleged in case number 502878, although the two cases should probably be consolidated. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-02-2011 Continued To Feb-03-12 At 02:00 P.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens And Permit Transfer Of The Property And For Attys Fees
Off Calendar Dec-16-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF DOUGLAS SMITH'S MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS AND PERMIT TRANSFER OF THE PROPERTY AND FOR ATTORNEYS FEES.
Appearances: Jonathan Fried (986.7000) for defendant and responding party; Carlos A. Alvarez of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP (421.3400) and Peter H. Bonis of the Law Offices of Peter H. Bonis (925.287.6428) for plaintiff and moving party; no other appearances.
This matter has been continued from December 15, 2011 for someone to appear on behalf of Mr. Anthony. There being no other appearances, the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiffs state the seven items of relief they request on page 9 of their memorandum filed with their moving papers. Plaintiffs are entitled to an expungement of the lis pendens (item 5) since defendant 300 Parking Street LLC states that it no longer is the owner of the disputed note and that it has removed the lis pendens. All other items of relief sought by plaintiffs are denied. Plaintiffs provide no authority that this court is permitted to grant items 1, 2, 3, 4 and 6 absent a trial, a recognized dispositive motion, an order to show cause or a preliminary injunction motion and, even if the court could grant such relief, plaintiffs have not provided any admissible evidence to support the relief. The declaration of Mr. Bonis, while it states that is made on personal knowledge, quite obviously is not - at least as to the purported facts as to the asserted illegality of the disputed note. Plaintiffs are not able to recover theirfees for this motion (item 7), even the portion of those fees relating to the lis pendens since plaintiffs have provided no evidence of the amount of those fees. Plaintiffs' memorandum states that a "fee declaration" is "submitted separately, but the court has not received one. Notwithstanding the denial of all but item 5, it seems apparent that something is very wrong with regard to the disputed note and its serial assignments and that the parties and counsel are advised that they must scrupulously refrain from any fraudulent or other illegal conduct. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. The court issued a Temporary Restraining Order and Order to Show Cause as to Joseph Anthony, Note Purchasing Corporation and J. Anthony Investments and set a hearing for January 5, 2012 at 9:30 am in department 302. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Not Reported
